description_long: This is a small, single-engine, fixed-wing airplane with a bright yellow fuselage accented by white stripes and wingtips. The aircraft features a high-wing configuration, providing stability and good visibility below the wings, and is supported by a tricycle landing gear with two main wheels and a single nose wheel. The propeller is mounted at the nose, indicating a piston engine, and the cockpit is positioned at the front of the fuselage with a clear, enclosed canopy. The tail section is conventional, with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers extending from either side. The overall design suggests a light utility or trainer aircraft, suitable for short takeoff and landing operations. The surface appears smooth and hard, typical of metal or composite-skinned aircraft.
description: A small, yellow, high-wing airplane with a nose-mounted propeller, tricycle landing gear, and a conventional tail. The cockpit is forward and enclosed, with white accent stripes and wingtips.
description_view_0: Rear view showing the full span of the high-mounted yellow wings, the tail assembly, and the tricycle landing gear.
description_view_1: Oblique rear-side view highlighting the yellow fuselage, white stripes, and the single vertical stabilizer with horizontal stabilizers.
description_view_2: Front view displaying the nose-mounted propeller, forward cockpit, and the wide high-wing configuration with visible landing gear.
description_view_3: Oblique front-side view showing the propeller, cockpit canopy, and the main landing gear under the wings.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing, straight wings with slight dihedral and squared wingtips, no visible winglets.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle landing gear with two main wheels and a single nose wheel, fixed configuration.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a single canopy, likely single or dual seat.
